serialIsolation.reason: API documentation page checks source plugin documentation sync and localized runtime resources.
Logout verifies shared authenticated browser state and must not overlap with authenticated flows in the same worker state.
The login presentation assertions mutate the global login panel layout config.

Source-plugin-owned E2E tests may install, enable, sync, seed, and exercise their owning plugins while validating runtime i18n cache, hosted assets, permissions, tenant flows, or plugin-managed configuration.
SQLite business zero-impact validation installs, enables, disables, and uninstalls a source plugin against the shared runtime.
Plugin tests install, enable, disable, uninstall, upload, sync, and authorize plugins while touching plugin artifacts and generated menus.

Menu tests create and update shared menu and permission matrix data, authorization tests sync plugin menus before assigning permissions, and dynamic plugin menu regressions may build runtime artifacts.

Role tests create, edit, authorize, delete, and inspect shared role records, role-menu permission assignments, role-user relationships, and dictionary-backed data-scope options; CI E2E runs must not overlap these files with other admin-table flows.
User management tests create, import, export, reset passwords, assign roles, and read plugin-provided organization seed data while sharing the admin account and user table state.
Workspace quick navigation traverses shared host module routes and waits for table-backed pages that depend on the global menu, permission, and seed state.
The user-editable data i18n regression mutates the shared admin profile and validates localized projections against shared seed data.
Config metadata localization depends on shared runtime i18n bundles and system configuration metadata rendered in the admin table.
Menu title localization reads shared menu, permission, and plugin-governance entries through runtime i18n projections.
Built-in role localization reads shared role seed data and must not overlap with role mutation or authorization flows.
Runtime i18n disabled checks depend on global i18n configuration and runtime bundle visibility.
Role and user English seed display checks read shared role and user table projections under runtime i18n.
Language switching uses the authenticated admin shell and runtime i18n bundle updates, so it should not overlap with other admin-shell locale regressions.
Opened tab title relocalization depends on shared runtime i18n bundles and admin-shell locale state.
Runtime i18n switching verifies dynamic plugin language bundles and cache refresh behavior.
English runtime page audit enables source and dynamic plugins and validates translated runtime pages.
Editable master data localization checks enable source plugins and read their shared mock data under the English runtime locale.
Plugin static-label localization checks enable multiple source plugins and load their mock data before auditing English pages.
English layout checks enable the plugin-demo-source plugin and depend on runtime i18n resources.
Built-in governance localization checks read shared dictionary, scheduler, and source-plugin-provided audit/login log data while creating fresh audit records.
Runtime i18n ETag checks depend on shared translation bundle versions and cache revalidation semantics.
Backend error localization checks require source plugins to be installed and enabled before asserting localized errors.
Backend export localization checks enable multiple source plugins and validate localized export labels.
Backend hardcoded Chinese regression checks enable source plugins before probing localized backend surfaces.
Organization, dictionary, and config English layout regression enables the org-center source plugin and reads shared seed records across localized pages.
Built-in handler readonly checks assert source-plugin-managed cron projections such as server-monitor collection in addition to host built-ins.
Scheduler job tests mutate job data, shell execution config, plugin cascades, and generated log state.
System config tests mutate global runtime and frontend configuration values.
File management tests exercise shared upload artifacts, file rows, route authorization, and file data-permission boundaries.
Dictionary global-effect checks navigate to plugin-owned department pages from org-center after mutating shared dictionary labels.
Dictionary data panel toolbar checks select operation-log dictionary types seeded by the monitor-operlog source plugin.

Dictionary tests create, import, edit, export, and delete shared dictionary types and data; dictionary option permission regressions also prepare source plugin menus before assigning business-only permissions.
Settings-owned user tests validate user labels, batch edits, and data-permission behavior against shared user, role, and organization seed data.
